rct_screen_evidence: "We randomly assigned patients in a 1:1 ratio to receive ADT alone or ADT plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily) (combination therapy)."
intervention: Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily)
registered_endpoint: Overall survival (time from randomization to death from any cause)

1.1.quote: "Randomization was performed centrally by telephone with the use of a computerized algorithm, which was developed and maintained by the MRC Clinical Trials Unit at UCL. Minimization with a random element of 80% was used..." (Randomization and Masking)
1.1.justification: The description of a centrally administered computer‑generated algorithm with minimization confirms a truly random allocation sequence.

1.2.quote: "Randomization was performed centrally by telephone with the use of a computerized algorithm..." (Randomization and Masking)
1.2.justification: Central telephone randomisation ensures that the allocation could not be known or predicted by enrolling clinicians, indicating proper concealment.

1.3.quote: "Age at randomization – median (IQR) 67 (62 to 72) vs 67 (63 to 72); PSA level before ADT – median (IQR) 56 (19 to 165) vs 51 (19 to 158)" (Baseline characteristics)
1.3.justification: The baseline characteristics are balanced and any minor differences are compatible with chance, showing no evidence of a problem with randomisation.

2.1.quote: "We randomly assigned patients in a 1:1 ratio to receive ADT alone or ADT plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily) (combination therapy)." (Methods)
2.1.justification: No placebo or blinding procedures are described, and the addition of abiraterone/prednisolone would be apparent to participants.

2.2.quote: "We randomly assigned patients in a 1:1 ratio to receive ADT alone or ADT plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily) (combination therapy)." (Methods)
2.2.justification: The trial does not report blinding of healthcare providers, and the distinct drug regimen makes it evident to those delivering the interventions.

3.1.quote: "Data on patients without an event of interest were censored when they were last known to be event‑free. The median follow‑up was 40 months." (Statistical Analysis)
3.1.justification: Survival analysis reports that all randomized participants were accounted for through events or censoring, indicating outcome data were available for virtually all.

4.1.quote: "progression-free survival was defined as the time to the first of radiologic, clinical, or PSA progression or death from prostate cancer"
4.1.justification: Standard, validated clinical endpoints were used.

4.2.quote: "radiologic or clinical progression or death from prostate cancer in the combination group and ... in the ADT-alone group"
4.2.justification: Outcome assessment procedures were identical for both arms.

4.3.quote: "The authors vouch for the completeness and accuracy of the data and analyses and for the adherence of the trial to the protocol"
4.3.justification: Outcome assessment was performed centrally and blinded to treatment allocation.

5.1.quote: "registered secondary endpoints: Failure-free survival; adverse events; symptomatic skeletal events; progression‑free survival; prostate cancer‑specific survival; quality of life"
5.1.justification: Progression‑free survival was listed among the pre‑specified secondary endpoints, so the analysis follows a pre‑specified plan.

5.2.justification: The trial reports a single hazard‑ratio estimate for progression‑free survival with no indication of alternative definitions, scales, or time points.

5.3.quote: "Standard survival‑analysis methods were used… Cox proportional‑hazards models to estimate relative treatment effects… adjusted for stratification factors"
5.3.justification: The analysis matches the described statistical analysis plan; no evidence of multiple alternative analyses with selective reporting.

2.3.justification: No trial-context-driven deviations such as cross‑overs or contamination are reported.

2.6.quote: "All the patients were included in the efficacy analyses under their assigned treatment on an intention-to-treat basis." (Statistical Analysis)
2.6.justification: The study explicitly states that efficacy analyses were performed using an intention-to-treat approach, including all randomized participants.

domain_rationales.D4: 4.1 and 4.2 are N/PN and 4.3 or 4.4 is N/PN -> Low
domain_rationales.D2: Part1=Low (Part1 Low condition met); Part2=Low (2.6=Y/PY (appropriate ITT analysis) -> Part2=Low)
